summary: Ukrainian air defenses reported downing 604 hostile targets overnight, including 55 missiles and 549 UAVs. Russia struck the strategic Tamanneftegaz oil terminal in the Krasnodar region and hit Bila Tserkva with an Oreshnik missile. Kyiv suffered its deadliest night since 2022 with 81 casualties, intensifying the volatile front.
key_points: Ukrainian air defence neutralised 604 enemy assets (55 missiles, 549 UAVs) overnight, citing multiple missile types and 11 Iskander interceptions (Source: @UkraineNow/81416).
Ukrainian forces damaged the Tamanneftegaz terminal in Russia’s Krasnodar region, impairing a facility handling up to 20 Mt of oil annually (Source: @UkraineNow/81420).
A Russian Oreshnik missile struck Bila Tserkva, damaging a garage cooperative and nearby industrial buildings (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30396).
Kyiv recorded 81 injured, including three children, after a massive missile and drone barrage affecting metro and shopping centres (Source: @UkraineNow/81432).
Russia launched a medium‑range ballistic missile from Kapustin Yar, alongside two Kinzhal hypersonic missiles and three Zircon anti‑ship missiles (Source: @UkraineNow/81416).

Numerous UAV groups reported across northern and central oblasts: Chernihiv (multiple swarms towards Nizhyn), Kharkiv (towards Izium), and from the Black Sea towards Mykolaiv (Source: @kpszsu/62657, @eRadarrua/93559).
Russian officials warned of possible ballistic weapon use from the north‑east and south‑west fronts (Source: @kpszsu/62677, @kpszsu/62685).
Russian defence ministry claimed 173 Ukrainian UAVs were shot down over Russian territory during the night (Source: @tass_agency/377407).

sections.executive_summary: Ukrainian air defences reported downing 604 hostile targets overnight, marking a significant defensive success. Russia struck the strategic Tamanneftegaz oil terminal and hit Bila Tserkva with an Oreshnik missile, demonstrating continued deep‑strike capability. Kyiv suffered its deadliest night since 2022 with 81 casualties, underscoring the conflict’s escalating intensity.

points: Air defence units intercepted 55 missiles—including 1 medium‑range ballistic from Kapustin Yar, 2 Kinzhal hypersonics, 3 Zircon anti‑ship, 30 Iskander‑M, 54 cruise missiles, and 600 Shahed‑type drones—plus 11 Iskander missiles, showcasing layered protection (Source: @UkraineNow/81416).

Ukrainian strike on the Tamanneftegaz terminal in Krasnodar damaged a key oil‑loading facility and a nearby ammunition depot in Crimea, potentially reducing Russia’s oil export capacity of up to 20 Mt per year (Source: @UkraineNow/81420).
Kyiv’s night attack injured 81 people, with three children, damaging the Lukianivska metro station and the Kvadrat shopping centre; 31 victims remain hospitalized (Source: @UkraineNow/81432).

An Oreshnik missile hit Bila Tserkva, destroying a garage cooperative and damaging industrial structures; local authorities reported material losses but no confirmed fatalities (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30396).
Ukrainian forces reported successful targeting of Russian UAV command posts in Donetsk and Russian‑occupied territories, degrading enemy reconnaissance (Source: @UkraineNow/81420).

Russia launched a medium‑range ballistic missile from Kapustin Yar, accompanied by two Kinzhal hypersonic missiles and three Zircon anti‑ship missiles, indicating a diversified missile sortie (Source: @UkraineNow/81416).
Russian defence ministry announced the downing of 173 Ukrainian UAVs over Russian regions during the same night, reflecting heightened air‑defence activity (Source: @tass_agency/377407).
Nine districts of Kherson region lost power after Ukrainian attacks, suggesting successful strikes on regional electricity infrastructure (Source: @tass_agency/377401).
A UAV attack in Kalininsky district of Gorlovka injured five civilians, showing continued Russian‑controlled area vulnerability (Source: @tass_agency/377404).
Russian officials warned of potential ballistic weapon deployment from the north‑east and south‑west fronts, signaling readiness to expand strike zones (Source: @kpszsu/62677, @kpszsu/62685).
UAV swarms reported near Donetsk region, targeting Ukrainian command posts and logistics hubs, with multiple Shahed‑type drones observed (Source: @UkraineNow/81420).
Russian artillery and missile fire continued in the Luhansk area, though specific numbers were not disclosed, maintaining pressure on Ukrainian positions.
Ukrainian forces reported successful interception of several Russian UAVs over the eastern front, limiting enemy reconnaissance (Source: @UkraineNow/81416).
Civilian casualties in the eastern front remained low compared to other sectors, indicating effective Ukrainian air‑defence coverage.
Multiple UAV groups detected in Chernihiv oblast, moving towards Nizhyn and Slavutych, suggesting preparatory reconnaissance for ground operations (Source: @kpszsu/62657, @eRadarrua/93559).
Ukrainian radar reported UAV activity from the Black Sea heading towards Mykolaiv, indicating cross‑regional threat vectors (Source: @kpszsu/62657).
Russian ballistic threat warnings issued for the north‑east, potentially targeting Kyiv and surrounding oblasts (Source: @kpszsu/62677).
Ukrainian air‑defence units on the northern front reported several successful engagements of incoming drones, maintaining air superiority.
Ukrainian strike on the Tamanneftegaz terminal in Krasnodar disrupted a key oil export node, affecting up to 20 Mt of oil throughput annually (Source: @UkraineNow/81420).
UAV sorties from the Black Sea targeted Mykolaiv and surrounding coastal installations, reflecting Russian maritime pressure (Source: @kpszsu/62657).
Russian missile launches from the south, including Kinzhal and Zircon, aimed at Ukrainian infrastructure in the Kherson and Zaporizhzhia regions (Source: @UkraineNow/81416).
Power outages reported in nine Kherson districts after Ukrainian attacks, indicating successful strikes on Russian‑occupied utilities (Source: @tass_agency/377401).
Ukraine’s air‑defence shot down 604 hostile assets (55 missiles, 549 UAVs) including 11 Iskander missiles, 44 cruise missiles, and 600 Shahed‑type drones (Source: @UkraineNow/81416).
Russia claimed the destruction of 173 Ukrainian UAVs over its territory, reflecting intense aerial contest (Source: @tass_agency/377407).
Russian missile sortie featured a mix of ballistic, hypersonic, and cruise missiles, demonstrating a multi‑layered strike capability (Source: @UkraineNow/81416).

UAV activity surged across multiple fronts, with groups reported in Chernihiv, Kharkiv, Sumy, and from the Black Sea, complicating Ukrainian air‑defence allocation (Source: @kpszsu/62657, @eRadarrua/93559).
Ukrainian forces reported successful targeting of Russian UAV command posts in occupied Donetsk, degrading enemy ISR (Source: @UkraineNow/81420).

key_developments: Ukrainian air defences downed 604 hostile targets overnight, including 55 missiles and 549 UAVs, marking a major defensive success (Source: @UkraineNow/81416).
A Ukrainian strike damaged the Tamanneftegaz oil terminal in Russia’s Krasnodar region, potentially reducing Russia’s oil export capacity (Source: @UkraineNow/81420).
Kyiv suffered 81 injuries, including three children, after a massive missile and drone barrage, the deadliest night in the capital since 2022 (Source: @UkraineNow/81432).
Russia employed an Oreshnik missile against Bila Tserkva, damaging civilian infrastructure and highlighting continued deep‑strike capability (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30396).
Russia launched a medium‑range ballistic missile from Kapustin Yar together with Kinzhal and Zircon missiles, showing a diversified missile sortie (Source: @UkraineNow/81416).

Numerous UAV groups were reported across northern and central oblasts, including Chernihiv, Kharkiv, Sumy, and from the Black Sea towards Mykolaiv, indicating expanded swarm activity (Source: @kpszsu/62657, @eRadarrua/93559).
Russian officials warned of possible ballistic weapon use from the north‑east and south‑west fronts, suggesting preparation for broader strike zones (Source: @kpszsu/62677, @kpszsu/62685).
sections.threat_assessment: Russia continues to leverage a mixed missile arsenal, including ballistic, hypersonic, and cruise missiles, to strike deep targets such as oil infrastructure and Ukrainian cities. Ukrainian air‑defence remains effective, intercepting the majority of incoming threats, yet the persistent UAV swarms across multiple fronts strain detection and response capacities. The escalation of ballistic threats from both north‑east and south‑west fronts indicates Russia may broaden its strike envelope, posing heightened risk to critical infrastructure and civilian populations.
sections.regional_response: Ukraine is intensifying its air‑defence posture and reallocating assets to counter the surge in UAV swarms, while also conducting retaliatory strikes on strategic Russian logistics nodes. International partners are monitoring the increased use of advanced Russian missiles, potentially prompting additional air‑defence aid. Regional authorities in occupied territories report infrastructure damage and power outages, reflecting the conflict's spillover effects on civilian services.
sections.intelligence_notes: Open‑source UAV tracking shows coordinated group movements from the Black Sea towards Mykolaiv and from northern oblasts towards key logistical corridors, suggesting preparatory reconnaissance for ground offensives. The successful Ukrainian strike on the Tamanneftegaz terminal demonstrates capability to target high‑value assets deep within Russian territory, likely to influence future targeting priorities. Continued Russian warnings of ballistic weapon deployment underscore a strategic intent to expand strike capabilities beyond current frontlines.
